name: KMS Tareq
title: Graduate Teaching Assistant
biography: KMS Tareq is a doctoral researcher and a fractional teacher at SOAS. His research investigates the effectiveness of international and domestic legal mechanisms in addressing climate change-related human mobility in Bangladesh. Before joining SOAS, Tareq was serving as an Associate Professor (Reader) of Law, University of Rajshahi, Bangladesh. He obtained LLB Honours and LLM degrees from the same institution. For securing extraordinary results both in undergraduate and postgraduate examinations, Tareq was awarded Prime Minister as well as University Gold Medals. Tareq also attained an LLM in International Law (distinction) degree from the University of Keele, UK on a Commonwealth Scholarship. Tareq is interested in international law, international environmental law, climate change law, and international refugee law. He has taught a range of Law modules including International Law, International Environmental Law, and Tort Law.
